Title: Revolutionizing AI Performance and Image Generation with Ollama's MLX-Powered Update — March 31, 2026

Ollama revolutionizes local AI capabilities with groundbreaking updates: **MLX-powered performance on Apple Silicon**, seamless **image generation**, and simplified workflows via `

Sponsored

Sponsor slot — sponsors@forgecore.co

Title: Revolutionizing AI Performance and Image Generation with Ollama's MLX-Powered Update — March 31, 2026

Hook

Ollama revolutionizes local AI capabilities with groundbreaking updates: MLX-powered performance on Apple Silicon, seamless image generation, and simplified workflows via ollama launch. These advancements enable users to run advanced AI models—like Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Z-Image Turbo—locally or via cloud, without configuration hassles.

Top Story

Ollama's latest updates focus on performance optimization, new features, and integration with AI tools. Key developments include:

1. Performance Boost: Ollama now leverages Apple's MLX framework for Apple Silicon devices (M5, M5 Pro, M5 Max), improving AI workload execution speed by up to 30%. 2. Enhanced GPU Neural Accelerators: Faster TTFT and generation speeds on Apple's latest chips. 3. Simplified Setup: Launches coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex with local or cloud models, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files. 4. Compatibility: Codex CLI supports open models like gpt-oss:20b or gpt-oss:120b. 5. Cloud Models: All Ollama Cloud models work with Codex (e.g., gpt-oss:120b-cloud). 6. Functionality: Connects messaging tools and automates tasks, streamlining the path from idea to implementation while maintaining model flexibility.

Why It Matters

  • Teams gain more value when AI is integrated into specific workflows instead of abstract mandates, leading to increased productivity and cost savings.
  • Local and hybrid deployments matter for privacy, latency, or repeatability considerations in purchasing decisions, making Ollama an attractive choice for businesses prioritizing these factors.
  • Operators need evidence, processes, and measurable outcomes before incorporating a tool into their stack. With Ollama's updates, they can test the benefits of local AI processing and image generation on their workflows.

Highlights

  • Performance Boost: Ollama now leverages Apple's MLX framework for Apple Silicon devices (M5, M5 Pro, M5 Max), improving AI workload execution speed by up to 30%.
  • Enhanced GPU Neural Accelerators: Faster TTFT and generation speeds on Apple's latest chips.
  • Simplified Setup: Launches coding tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex with local or cloud models, eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.
  • Compatibility: Codex CLI supports open models like gpt-oss:20b or gpt-oss:120b.
  • Cloud Models: All Ollama Cloud models work with Codex (e.g., gpt-oss:120b-cloud).
  • Functionality: Connects messaging tools and automates tasks, streamlining the path from idea to implementation while maintaining model flexibility.

Tool of the Week

Claude Code with Ollama offers a powerful combination for AI-focused operators, providing a flexible tool for implementing advanced models in specific workflows.

Workflow

# 1) Choose an existing workflow
ollama list

# 2) Define your success metric before rollout
echo "Measure time saved, error rate, and cycle time"

# 3) Pilot with one team and review results weekly
echo "Promote only if the workflow is repeatable"

CTA

Test a workflow from this issue, define your success metric, pilot it with one team, and review results weekly. Only promote the workflow if the benefits are sustainable and provide tangible improvements to your operations.
